commit.message:
feat: day-trader signal engine (VWAP + ATR + EMA ribbon + volume)

Confluence engine upgrades:
- EMA ribbon (5/8/21 bars) replaces MA15/MA60 crossover
  Full alignment (5>8>21 or 5<8<21) scores +2; partial cross scores +1
  More granular trend strength vs binary old MA cross
- VWAP (Volume Weighted Average Price) — session anchor resets each day
  Price side (above/below VWAP) contributes +1 to SELL or BUY score
  VWAP crossover (fresh) contributes additional +1
- ATR(14) dynamic take-profit threshold (replaces fixed 1%)
  Take-profit = entry + 1.5× ATR, scales with current volatility
- Volume confirmation: bar with volume ≥ 2× 20-bar avg adds +1
  to whichever direction signal already has points (filters noise)
- Score threshold raised from >=2 to >=3 (more conditions = more agreement)
- Confidence thresholds: LOW(3) / MEDIUM(4) / HIGH(5+) [was 2/3/4]
- Safety: EMA fully bearish penalises BUY by -1 (buying into downtrend)

Force output (--force / -f) now shows:
- EMA5/8/21 values and ribbon direction (BULLISH/BEARISH/MIXED)
- VWAP and price distance from VWAP
- ATR take-profit and stop-loss levels
- Volume ratio vs average

instruments.json: added ema_fast_bars, ema_mid_bars, ema_slow_bars,
  atr_period_bars, atr_take_profit_mult, volume_lookback_bars, volume_spike_mult

README: updated Signal Engines section with scoring table, indicator
  comparison (old vs new), config keys reference, and new Telegram format

Update remote-host-object.md with SDK .tlb shipping and IDispatch example (#13324)

Update `remote-host-object.md` with documentation for the MSBuild
apphost changes.

### What's added

1. **Shipping `Microsoft.Build.Framework.tlb` with the .NET SDK** -
documents that the `.tlb` ships starting from 10.0.3xx, explains why
it's needed (COM marshaling for `ITaskHost` across process boundaries),
what capabilities it enables (ROT host objects, registration-free COM,
parity with VS), and shows the SDK layout.

2. **Practical `IDispatch` example** - end-to-end example showing how a
VS host object (WebTools) serializes task items to JSON via
`QueryAllTaskItems`, and how the SDK Container task
([dotnet/sdk#52856](https://github.com/dotnet/sdk/pull/52856)) calls it
via `IDispatch` (reflection `InvokeMember`) with a fallback to the
legacy in-proc path.

Related PRs:
- dotnet/msbuild#13175 (Add App Host Support for MSBuild)
- dotnet/sdk#52856 (Refactor VSHostObject for COM compatibility)
- [WebTools PR
701336](https://dev.azure.com/devdiv/DevDiv/_git/WebTools/pullrequest/701336)

commit.message:
fix(formBuilder): ensure cloned choices and rows ids are unique DEV-1759 (#6740)

### 📣 Summary

When cloning a question in Form Builder the result is now ensured to be
a copy with unique ids. Previously clone was using original row ids
leading to unexpected behaviour.

### 💭 Notes

The problem was happening for every cloned row. The `$kuid` was being
cloned too. Luckily we don't rely on `$kuid` much, but it is an obvious
error that could be potentially problematic.

The visible issue was happening for cloning of few types: `select_one`,
`select_multiple`, `rank` or `score`. When cloning such row, code was
also cloning the list of choices for it. Before the fix the cloned list
of choices was wrongly being synced between two rows (the original and
the clone). This leads to confusing issues as the syncing isn't obvious
and might get unnoticed and lead to unwanted changes in the form.

Changes in this PR:
- In `clone` function of choices list
(`jsapp/xlform/src/model.choices.coffee`), I ensure that the unique ids
are stripped and regenerated - for the list itself and for each option.
I also register the new list in global list of `choices`
- In `clone` (look for `rr.clone`) function of `ScoreRankMixin`
(`jsapp/xlform/src/model.row.coffee`) I similarly ensure unique ids are
stripped and regenerated - for the row itself and for the score/rank
levels
- In `clone` function of `row.Row` (`jsapp/xlform/src/model.row.coffee`)
I introduced safety measure to ensure unique properties are not being
copied and `select_one` and `select_multiple` are properly copying only
non-unique things
- Added one `TODO` comment for a weird single use `_isCloned` flag
- Added comment about us purposefuly not allowing `kobomatrix` cloning
(as I thought initially it was a mistake/bug)
- Some minor code readability improvements (coffee syntax is not nice)
- Added some comments here and there :) 

### 👀 Preview steps

I recommend this setup:
- Create new empty form and save (unselect "start date" and "end date"
in sidebar
- Open two tabs
- First tab at `http://kf.local.kbtdev.org/#/forms/<uid>/edit`
- Second tab at `http://kf.local.kbtdev.org/#/forms/<uid>/json`

1. `/edit` tab → Add `select_one` row with one choice
2. `/edit` tab → Save
3. `/json` tab → Refresh and notice `survey` has one item, and `choices`
have one item
4. `/edit` tab → Use "Duplicate Question" icon button (blue one on right
side of row)
5. `/edit` tab → Save
6. `/json` tab → Refresh and notice `survey` has two items, and
`choices` have two items:
1. 🔴 [on main] notice that two `survey` and two `choice` items have
identical `$kuid`s (they all should be unique) and
`select_from_list_name` (pairing with `list_name`) is pointing to the
same list (second item should point to different list)
2. 🟢 [on main] notice that two `survey` and two `choice` items have
different `$kuid`s and `select_from_list_name` are pointing to different
lists

Do a similar test with cloning:
- a `select_mulitple` question (test is identical to `select_one`, but
we need to test it separately, as code paths are not identical)
- a `text` question (`$kuid`s should be unique)
- a `rank` question (`$kuid`s should be unique, `kobo--rank-items`
should be pointing to different `list_name`)
- a `score` question (same as `rank`, but `kobo--score-choices` is the
name of property that should point to `list_name`)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Run all device IO tests both on silicon and TTSim (#2233)

### Issue
#2185

### Description
Refactors `test_device_io.cpp` to introduce a `TestDeviceIOFixture`
GTest fixture that transparently creates a `Cluster` for silicon or
TTSim simulation based on the `TT_UMD_SIMULATOR` environment variable.
When the env var is set, simulation options (`chip_type`,
`target_devices`, `simulator_directory`) are injected into any
`ClusterOptions` passed by the test, so custom options (e.g.
`num_host_mem_ch_per_mmio_device`) are preserved.

### List of the changes

* Added `TestDeviceIOFixture` with `make_cluster(ClusterOptions = {})`
helper and `is_simulation()` predicate.
* Converted all `TEST(TestDeviceIO, ...)` to
`TEST_F(TestDeviceIOFixture, ...)` so every test runs on both silicon
and TTSim.
* Removed TTSim-specific duplicate tests: `TTSimReadWriteL1` and
`TTSimSysmemReadWrite`.
* Replaced the parameterized `ClusterReadWriteL1Test` /
`INSTANTIATE_TEST_SUITE_P` with a plain `TEST_F(TestDeviceIOFixture,
ReadWriteL1)`.
* Merged silicon and TTSim `SysmemReadWrite` logic into a single test,
branching on `is_simulation()` for channel count, fill pattern, and test
offsets.
* Removed all `GTEST_SKIP` guards that checked for missing chips.
* Removed `ClusterReadWriteL1Test` from `test_api_common.hpp`.

### Testing
Run without `TT_UMD_SIMULATOR` for silicon, set `TT_UMD_SIMULATOR` for
TTSim simulation.

### API Changes
/

commit.message:
fix(image): remove filemd5 from triggers_replace; fix debug stdout buffering

ROOT CAUSE of CDI timeout: filemd5(var.local_image_path) reads the entire
image file at apply time when Terraform evaluates triggers_replace. For a
multi-GB image this blocks terraform_data.upload_image from starting for
1-2+ minutes — by which point CDI's countdown has nearly expired, so the
upload script starts but CDI immediately times out.

Fix: use var.local_image_path (path string) as the trigger instead. Still
triggers replacement when the path changes. For content changes at the same
path, taint manually: terraform taint 'terraform_data.upload_image[0]'.

Also fix HARVESTER_UPLOAD_DEBUG output buffering: bash block-buffers stdout
when not connected to a TTY (Terraform local-exec uses a pipe). Add
'exec 1>&2' in debug mode to redirect stdout to stderr, which is
line-buffered, so all output is visible in real time.

commit.message:
DX-2429: unify Run API with StreamRun, typed chunks, and private internals (#68)

* feat!: unify Run API with StreamRun, typed chunks, and private internals

Redesign the Run system to separate streaming from non-streaming runs
at the type level, yield typed Chunk objects instead of raw strings,
and encapsulate all internal state behind private fields.

BREAKING CHANGES:

- `Run` no longer implements `AsyncIterable`. Streaming methods now
  return `StreamRun`, a new subclass that implements `AsyncIterable<C>`.
  Code doing `for await (const x of run)` on a non-streaming run will
  no longer compile.

- `agent.stream()` returns `Promise<StreamRun<string, Chunk>>` instead
  of `Promise<Run<string>>`. The iterator now yields `Chunk` objects
  (discriminated union: text-delta, tool-call, reasoning, start, finish,
  stats) instead of plain strings. Consumers must check `chunk.type`
  to extract text: `if (chunk.type === "text-delta") chunk.text`.

- `exec.stream()` and `exec.streamCode()` return
  `Promise<StreamRun<string, ExecStreamChunk>>`. The iterator yields
  `ExecStreamChunk` objects (`{ type: "output", data }` or
  `{ type: "exit", exitCode, cpuNs }`) instead of plain strings.

- `run.status` is now a synchronous getter (returns cached status)
  instead of an async method that polls the backend.

- `run.type` uses `"command"` instead of `"shell"` for exec runs.

- `exec.code()` returns `Run<string>` instead of `CodeExecutionResult`.
  Use `run.result`, `run.exitCode`, `run.status` instead of
  `result.output`, `result.exit_code`.

- All internal `_`-prefixed fields on `Run` and `StreamRun` are now
  private. Use the public getters (`id`, `status`, `result`, `exitCode`,
  `cost`) instead. SDK internals use `Run._update()` and
  `StreamRun._setIterator()` static methods.

- `Box._run()` and `Box._stream()` are now private methods.

Other changes:
- New export: `StreamRun` from `@upstash/box`
- CLI `handleRun` now iterates Chunk objects directly, eliminating the
  pendingEvents queue workaround
- Added missing `await` on rejects assertion in cd integration test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use `run.exitCode` instead of `run.status` for shell exit code checks (#69)

* Initial plan

* fix: finalize StreamRun state on early termination and errors

Add try/catch/finally to all streaming iterators (_stream, _execStream,
_execStreamCode) so that Run state is always updated:

commit.message:
feat: zero-serialization WAL binary streaming via PipeWriter (#1332) (#1344)

Implement binary length-prefixed WAL streaming endpoint at GET /bmw/wal/stream.

Wire format:
  [count:uint32] repeat: [length:uint32] [wal record bytes...]

- WalStreamWriter.StreamAllAsync: streams entire HeadMap via CopyArrays
- WalStreamWriter.StreamEntityAsync: streams per-entity records via QueryBinary
- Endpoint accepts ?entity=X query param for filtered streaming
- Zero intermediate buffers — WAL payloads written directly to PipeWriter
- Back-pressure via periodic FlushAsync every 64 records
- Records streamed as-is (may be compressed/encrypted per WAL codec)

5 tests covering: empty store, multi-record framing, unknown entity,
cancellation, and large payload correctness.

Closes #1332

commit.message:
fix: echo back client message_id in SendMessage response

Server was generating a new UUID unconditionally, breaking delivery receipts.
Per proto contract (SendMessageResponse.message_id comment: 'echo back'),
the server must return the same ID the client sent.

Priority:
  1. envelope.message_id (from Envelope.message_id_type oneof)
  2. request.idempotency_key (if provided and non-empty)
  3. generated UUID (fallback only when client sends no ID)

Fixes:
- Delivery receipts not matching (recipient acks server UUID, sender
  looks up client UUID → never finds it)
- 'Delivered' status never set on sender side
- Potential re-delivery on reconnect due to unresolved ACK

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove custom ui_* overrides that broke keyboard in exports

Custom ui_* actions with physical_keycode:0/keycode:0 were overriding
Godot's built-in defaults. Godot's built-in ui_accept/ui_cancel/ui_left/
ui_right/ui_up/ui_down already map Enter, Space, arrows, and Escape
correctly using keycode (not physical_keycode). The custom overrides
caused Enter and arrow keys to stop working in Windows exports while
Space and Tab (hardcoded by engine) still worked.

commit.message:
fix(image): single curl per poll, 1 s sleep to cut CDI detection latency

The previous polling loop made two sequential curl calls per iteration
(one for the HTTP status code, a second for the body). Each is a full
TLS handshake; with a 2 s sleep between iterations the worst-case lag
between CDI reporting Initialized and the upload starting was:
  2× TLS round-trips + up to 2 s sleep ≈ several seconds

Replace with a single curl per iteration using -w '\nHTTPSTATUS:%{code}'
to embed the HTTP status as the last line of the response body, then
parse both code and body from that one response. Also halve the sleep
from 2 s to 1 s (MAX_WAIT raised from 150 to 300 to keep the 5-minute
timeout window).

Result: when CDI flips to Initialized, the upload starts within ~1 s
and with zero extra TLS overhead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix black screen when installed in Program Files on Windows (#93)

WebView2 defaults to creating its user data folder next to the executable.
When installed in C:\Program Files\, the process lacks write permissions,
causing WebView2 initialization to fail silently (black screen after splash).

Set the WebView2 user data folder to %LocalAppData%\MauiSherpa\WebView2
so the app works regardless of install location.

Fixes #89
